page: Amazon Web Services r6id.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It features 2 vCPUs (1 physical core with 2 threads) running at 3.5 GHz, backed by 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. This configuration yields a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1881.78 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1618.0, both falling into the average tier. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively low. The stress-ng multi-core score of 1923.39 and Geekbench multi-core score of 1855.0 are positioned in the bottom decile, reflecting the physical limitation of having only a single physical core. Memory bandwidth tests also show lower-tier performance, with large block read and write speeds measuring 15.03 GB/sec and 13.65 GB/sec respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r6id.large offers a specialized balance of high memory capacity and fast local NVMe storage at a lower entry point than larger instances in the same family. However, the primary tradeoff is the constrained processing throughput and limited memory bandwidth under heavy concurrent loads. This instance is best suited for workloads that require a larger memory footprint and low-latency local scratch space but do not demand intensive parallel computation, such as small-scale databases, caching layers like Redis, or memory-bound utility applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core execution, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1618.0, but poor multi-core and memory bandwidth results due to its single physical core architecture. Qualitatively, the instance provides a cost-effective entry point for memory-heavy workloads requiring local high-speed scratch space, though it presents a tradeoff in parallel processing power. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications with low parallel compute requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute needs.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM
118 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
Average single-core performance paired with limited multi-core scaling
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Well-suited for small-scale databases, caching, and memory-bound tasks
